---
title: "[INSERT TITLE]"
author: "Ian Cheong"
date: 2023-03-07
date-format: long
format:
  revealjs:
    embed-resources: true
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    incremental: true
    scrollable: true
    preview-links: true
    pointer:
      color: 'red'
    theme: [default, oicr.scss]
execute: 
  echo: false
  eval: true
slide-number: c
revealjs-plugins:
  - pointer
---

## Medulloblastoma (MB)

:::{style="width: 70%;"}
- Cancer of the hindbrain
- One of the most common malignant pediatric brain tumours
- Groups 3 and 4 are the least well understood, most prevalent
  - [Arise from progenitor cells in the rhombic lip (RL)]{style="font-size:smaller;"}
  - [Lack of animal/cell models, especially for Group 4]{style="font-size:smaller;"}
  - [Lack of targeted molecular therapies]{style="font-size:smaller;"}
- Need to develop better models and treatment
:::

![](../common/mb_subtypes-20240128.png){.absolute top=5% right=2% style="max-height:90%;"}


:::{.coverup style="top:5%; right:2%; width:0.45%; height:820px;"}
:::

::: {.citation}
Cavalli et al., Cancer Cell (2017)
:::

## Group 4 MB arises from the RL<sup>SVZ</sup>

::: {.absolute layout="[65,35]" top=22% left=0%}

![](../common/g4mb_deconvolution-20240128.png)

![](../common/mb_initiation-20240128.png){.fragment fragment-index=2}

:::

::: {.fragment .red-border fragment-index=1 style="top:28%; left:13%; height:32%; width:6.8%;"}
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index=2}

[RL<sup>VZ</sup>]{.absolute top=20% left=67% style="color:#f8ba57; font-size:0.75em;"}

[RL<sup>SVZ</sup>]{.absolute top=20% left=74% style="color:#acc95a; font-size:0.75em;"}

[UBCs]{.absolute top=20% left=83% style="color:#00a101; font-size:0.75em;"}

:::

[Hendrikse et al., Nature (2022)]{.citation}

## Rhombic lip progenitor niche is expanded in humans

:::: {.fragment}

![](../common/haldipur2019_rl_mouse.png){.absolute top=10% left=0% width=75%}

::: {.absolute .nonincremental top=15% left=77%}

Mouse RL:

- Transient
- One zone

:::

::::

:::: {.fragment}

![](../common/haldipur2019_rl_human.png){.absolute top=40% left=0% width=75%}

::: {.absolute .nonincremental top=60% left=77%}

Human RL:

- Long-lived
- Two zones

:::

::::

[Haldipur et al., Science (2019)]{.citation}

---

::: {.center}
![](../common/biorender/summary_nosplit_tumour-20230619.png){.absolute top=5% width=65%}
:::

<!-- semi-transparent shape to cover part of the figure -->
::: {.fragment .fade-out .absolute top=10% left=24.5%}
<svg height="580" width="650">
    <polygon points="0,0 0,80 50,150 100,200 100,580 650,580 650,0" style="fill:#ffffff7f" />
</svg>
:::

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="70%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}

::: {.fragment}

[**HYPOTHESIS**]{.font-main-theme}

::: {.small-font}
Human RL<sup>SVZ</sup> differentiation contains gene regulatory networks (i.e., cell states) that have no homologous equivalent in mice and are dysregulated in Group 4 MB.
:::

:::

<br/>

::: {.fragment}

[**GOAL**]{.font-main-theme}

::: {.small-font}
To identify evolutionarily recent gene regulatory networks that normally control development and abnormally cause stalled differentiation
:::

:::

:::

::::

## Single-cell RNA-sequencing datasets

<br/>
<br/>

| **Species** |   **Timepoints**    |    **Region**     | **Cell Counts** |                **Source**                |
|-------------|:-------------------:|:-----------------:|:---------------:|:----------------------------------------:|
| Human       | 9 PCW to<br/>21 PCW | whole cerebellum* |     ~69,000     | Aldinger et al.,<br/>Nat Neurosci (2021) |
| Mouse       |     E10 to P14      | whole cerebellum* |     ~60,000     |    Vladoiu et al.,<br/>Nature (2019)     |

: {.table-striped tbl-colwidths="[11,20,18,20,28]"}

::: {.aside}
*Note: only the RL-derived cells were used for integration (~9,000 human cells and ~25 000 mouse cells)
:::
